ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general smoked fish class; no species-specific smoked cod entry confirmed. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No confirmed SNOMED CT code for smoked cod as a food substance; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of smoked cod is approximately 100 g, equivalent to one small fillet portion, commonly used in cooking or eaten as a protein component of a meal.

nutrient_source.title: Smoked Cod, Raw — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for smoked cod, raw (cr_id F03040033). All core macros and available micronutrients reflect the HPB record with confidence 0.95. Saturated_fat_g adapted from USDA FoodData Central NDB 15027 (smoked cod) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown.

gi_source.title: N/A — Smoked Cod contains negligible carbohydrates; GI not applicable
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index is not applicable for smoked cod as it contains 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Glycemic index field is set to 0 as a numeric sentinel indicating not applicable for this negligible-carbohydrate protein food. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition. University of Sydney GI Database consulted; no GI entry exists for this food as expected for a negligible-carbohydrate protein food. gi_category is set to not_applicable.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 655 mg per 100 g — exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension-safe foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit smoked cod due to its very high sodium content of 655 mg/100 g; if consumed, soaking in fresh water for several hours before cooking can reduce sodium load, and no additional salt or high-sodium sauces should be added.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar; high protein 18.8 g/100 g with no glycaemic impact
type2_diabetes.guidance: Smoked cod is suitable for people with Type 2 Diabetes as it contains no carbohydrate or sugar and will not raise blood glucose; however, sodium content should be managed by soaking before use and avoiding sweet glazes or sugar-based marinades.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium 655 mg/100 g raises cardiovascular risk; low fat 1.0 g and low saturated fat 0.2 g are favourable
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While smoked cod is low in total fat and saturated fat, its high sodium content of 655 mg/100 g is a significant cardiovascular concern; patients with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size, soak the fish to reduce sodium, and prefer fresh or unsalted cod as a lower-risk alternative.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sodium 655 mg/100 g far exceeds the 400 mg RA threshold; may worsen RA-related tissue swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Smoked cod is not recommended for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to its sodium content of 655 mg/100 g, which exceeds the 400 mg RA threshold and may exacerbate inflammation and fluid retention; fresh cod is preferred to retain omega-3 benefits without the sodium burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Smoked cod (raw) is a processed white fish product made from Atlantic cod (Gadus morhua) fillets that have been brined in salt and then cold-smoked to impart a distinctive smoky flavour while preserving the fish without fully cooking it. The fillets are boneless with a firm, dense, and flaky texture and a pale to golden-white colour. As a raw smoked product, it requires cooking before consumption. Nutritionally, smoked cod is a high-protein, very low-fat food providing 85 kcal, 18.8 g protein, and only 1.0 g fat per 100 g, with zero carbohydrate and zero sugar, making it glycaemically neutral and suitable for low-carbohydrate and ketogenic dietary patterns. Its primary clinical concern is its very high sodium content of 655 mg per 100 g, which makes it unsuitable for individuals with hypertension, rheumatoid arthritis, or cardiovascular disease without sodium-reduction preparation steps such as soaking in fresh water. Cholesterol is moderate at 49 mg/100 g. Smoked cod is a good source of lean protein and is widely used in European and international cuisines in dishes such as fish cakes, chowders, kedgeree, and pasta. It is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free.
common_user_queries: How much sodium is in smoked cod?
Is smoked cod good for weight loss?
How do I reduce the salt in smoked cod?
Is smoked cod high in protein?
Can I eat smoked cod if I have high blood pressure?
What is the difference between smoked cod and fresh cod?
How many calories in smoked cod?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F03040033) with a recorded confidence of 0.95. The record is marked as reviewed and canonical. All core macros and available micronutrients are present and internally consistent.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero: smoked cod is a pure protein-and-fat fish product with no carbohydrate content; confirmed zero per HPB Foodo database. Sugar_g is confirmed zero by the same source. Dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero as fish contains no dietary fibre — naturally zero. Saturated_fat_g estimated at 0.2 g based on USDA FoodData Central entry for smoked cod (NDB 15027) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown; this is an adapted value. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile not reported in HPB Foodo for this entry; not available in primary source as of 2026. Vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrients are not reported in the HPB Foodo entry for smoked cod, raw, and detailed values are not available in the primary source as of 2026. Glycemic index field is set to numeric 0 as a required numeric sentinel value; gi_category is set to not_applicable because this food contains 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g and glycemic index is not meaningful for negligible-carbohydrate protein foods. Smoked cod is not a Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese dish; therefore no mandatory non-English local-language names are required under G6 multilingual rules.

q1_food_identity.notes: Canonical English food name 'Smoked Cod, Raw' is precise and unambiguous. No mandatory non-English local-language names required as this is a Western/international food product, not a Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese dish.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: All four core macros present: energy_kcal 85.0, protein_g 18.8, fat_g 1.0, carbohydrate_g 0.0. Carbohydrate_g zero is justified as confirmed zero per HPB Foodo. Source clearly named as HPB Singapore Foodo (cr_id F03040033, confidence 0.95, reviewed canonical record).

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g 0.0 (confirmed zero — fish contains no dietary fibre, naturally zero), sodium_mg 655.0, saturated_fat_g 0.2 (adapted from USDA FoodData Central NDB 15027), sugar_g 0.0 (confirmed zero per HPB). All four required secondary nutrients present as numeric values.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index set to numeric 0 as required by the validator (must be numeric). gi_category set to not_applicable as this food contains 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g. glycemic_load_per_serving set to 0.0. gi_source documents University of Sydney GI Database consultation confirming no GI entry exists for this negligible-carbohydrate food. All four GI fields present and numeric as required.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn closest_parent match to smoked fish food product class documented with gap note. FoodEx2 code A00LC for Cod, smoked used as exact match. SNOMED CT omitted with honest gap note. At least one defensible ontology anchor present (FoodEx2 exact match).

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is over 1,100 characters, well above the 200-character minimum. Covers food identity, preparation method, taste and texture, nutritional profile, clinical relevance (sodium, diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular), and culinary uses.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (high concern, sodium 655 mg), Type 2 Diabetes (low concern, zero carbohydrate), Cardiovascular Disease (moderate concern, high sodium vs low fat). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. Rheumatoid arthritis included as additional condition. Type 2 Diabetes correctly named with machine key type2_diabetes.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: Seven English aliases captured including alternate names, descriptive variants, and preparation-style variants. All aliases_by_language english entries verified to appear verbatim in flat aliases array. No mandatory non-English aliases required for this Western food product. aliases_by_language and flat aliases are fully consistent.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent' to align with allowed values for G5.
Iteration 2: Confirmed glycemic_index handling for negligible carbohydrate food.

Iteration 3: Changed glycemic_index from null to numeric 0 to satisfy validator requirement that glycemic_index must be numeric. Updated gi_source notes to clarify that 0 is used as a numeric sentinel value with gi_category not_applicable to indicate GI is not meaningful for this food. Updated notes field to document this handling.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Smoked Cod, Raw (F03040033) passes all six hard gates and scores 8/8 on the Q1-Q8 scorecard. The key fix in iteration 3 was setting glycemic_index to numeric 0 (rather than null) to satisfy the validator requirement that glycemic_index must be numeric, while gi_category remains not_applicable to correctly signal that GI is not meaningful for this negligible-carbohydrate protein food. Nutrient values are sourced from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical record (confidence 0.95, reviewed). Zero carbohydrate, sugar, and dietary fibre are all confirmed zero with justification. All -1 sentinel fields are documented in notes. Ontology uses a defensible FoodEx2 exact match and a FoodOn closest_parent match. Health context covers all three required conditions with specific clinical guidance. LLM description exceeds 200 characters. Alias consistency between aliases_by_language and flat aliases verified.
